_doc: Gated 6-tier LLM router policy for BOTH apps (Jarvis tiered_llm + Underworld tools/llm). The rule: route by TASK, run the cheapest tier that fits, escalate ONLY on a real failure — never preemptively, never by percentage. Most work must die at Tier 0 (no LLM). Heavy tiers stay cold and are hard-gated by VRAM.

0_none.use: ingest, metrics, dedup-by-id/hash, routing, db upserts, dashboard, glb-load, queue scheduling

1_micro.use: tiny classification, JSON cleanup, tag/title rewrite, yes/no routing

2_base.use: DEFAULT LLM — normal enrichment, short summaries, basic descriptions

3_strong.use: complex reasoning, technical synthesis, multi-step planning, code analysis, hard ontology merges

4_heavy.use: rare difficult multi-doc synthesis — load-on-demand / burst GPU only

5_extreme.use: critical code repair, architecture, repeated lower-tier failure, human-requested premium

principle: Decide the task TYPE first; pick the cheapest tier that completes it.
Escalate exactly ONE tier and ONLY on an explicit failure reason (error, empty output, low confidence).
Never escalate on vague uncertainty, never by a target percentage, never auto-escalate batch jobs to heavy.
Heavy (Tier 4/5) is cold by default and only runs when priority is high AND resources are healthy.

resource_gates.note: VRAM% is of the shared 2x4090 box (48GB). Gates apply to LOADING a non-resident model — a resident model is free to use.

residency_policy.rule: Keep at most two local tiers hot. 70B never resident on the 2x4090 — burst GPU or external only.

knowledge_builder.note: 70B is disabled for background knowledge batches until lower tiers are proven stable

self_learning.note: Tier 5 only on real repeated failure

underworld_overmind.note: 70B/god-brain is future/manual only; perfect sub-70B first

anti_escalation: Never 70B for: live measurements, dashboard/system metrics, GLB loading, basic OCR, dedup (unless deterministic+embedding both fail on a high-value entity).
Never run heavy tiers inside infinite loops.
Self-learning must not recursively call bigger models for every small error — dedup issues, one lesson per (module,kind).
